vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely invalidated by the current visual evidence. P1 (String of Nickels), P2 (Mexican Mint), and P4 (Silver Guest) are not visible as healthy specimens. P3 (Pothos) is represented only by the scale anchor (white rabbit) and a single, struggling sprout.

inventory_reconciliation.P1: Systemic Loss: No foliage detected in yellow pot.
inventory_reconciliation.P2: Systemic Loss: No foliage detected in black pot.
inventory_reconciliation.P3: Partial Presence: White rabbit anchor present; single green sprout observed.
inventory_reconciliation.P4: Systemic Loss: No foliage detected in black pot.

plant_audit.P1: Empty soil surface; no signs of life.
plant_audit.P2: Empty soil surface; no signs of life.
plant_audit.P3: One small, thin green sprout is visible in the soil near the white rabbit. The rest of the pot is bare.
plant_audit.P4: Empty soil surface; no signs of life.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Soil appears dark and potentially over-saturated or compacted.
biome_observations.debris: White rabbit anchor is present in P3. No other significant debris.
biome_observations.fungal_presence: None detected, though low light makes identification difficult.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t3: Initial state shows bare soil across all pots.
temporal_deltas.t3_to_t2: Minimal change; sprout in P3 remains static.
temporal_deltas.t2_to_t1: Sprout in P3 shows slight elongation.
temporal_deltas.t1_to_current: Lighting conditions have degraded significantly, making further observation of the sprout difficult.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of critical failure. With the exception of a single, isolated sprout in P3, all registered plants have suffered systemic loss. The lack of foliage suggests either extreme senescence, improper light levels, or total plant death.
vision_report.anomalies: The primary anomaly is the near-total absence of the registered flora. The presence of a single sprout in P3 is the only biological activity recorded.
vision_report.narrative_description: I have performed a meticulous audit of the provided image sequence. The biome is currently non-viable. The registry provided does not match the physical reality of the pots, which are almost entirely devoid of plant matter. The white rabbit anchor in P3 remains the only constant, accompanied by a single, fragile sprout that appears to be the sole survivor of the collection. The lighting in the final image is insufficient for further detailed botanical analysis.
